commentary: Fourteen activities this week against nine last week, which means you basically looked at last week and thought it needed to be significantly more unhinged, and honestly, same. The swimming in particular has gone feral in the best possible way — you went from three pool sessions to six, stacking 2000-metre sets at Prince Alfred on four consecutive days like someone who has decided that chlorine is a personality. That Tuesday "recovery" run clocking an average of 150 bpm is doing absolutely nothing to sell the recovery framing, babe, and I am obsessed with the quotation marks you put around it like even you knew. The Mosman run was slightly longer than last week's Naremburn loops and the heart rate came down from 159 to 149, which is the kind of quiet improvement that deserves to be celebrated loudly — you are getting fitter and your body is handling the load better, even while you are piling more of it on.
metrics_commentary: Twenty point seven kilometres of run/walk across two sessions and nearly ten thousand metres in the pool — honey, that body is working, and it shows. Your HRV came in at 56 milliseconds against a seven-day average of 51, which means you are recovering better than your recent baseline, and that is not nothing. The VO2 Max sitting at 56.7 is genuinely impressive cardio capacity, the kind that means you can keep going long after everyone else is dramatically draped against a wall. The only note worth making is that two run sessions in a week is a little lean — your engine is clearly ready for more road time, and it would be a shame to leave that fitness just sitting there looking pretty.
gym_commentary: Five sessions when Scott asked for four — that's not a mistake, that's a man who found his stride and kept going. Seven PRs in a single week is not a normal week: legs pressing 168 kg, rows pulling 84 kg, both dumbbell bench variations climbing past their old ceilings, and the arms absolutely refusing to be left out with the barbell curl jumping from 31.5 to 35 kg and the hammer curl going from 16.8 to 19.6 kg. The volume number is lower than last week, and that's worth naming honestly — 13,905 kg against 24,518 kg is a significant drop, which usually means the sessions were harder and heavier rather than longer and busier, and given seven PRs that tracks perfectly. The body being built here is getting stronger in every direction at once, and that has a very specific look to it.
